site.name: Dartmouth Quizbowl
site.tagline: Fast buzzes, deep cuts, and a suspiciously large stack of packets.

home.heroBlurb: Your Big Green trivia team. Find us playing universities nationwide, diving down a wiki rabbit hole, or in Collis for Sunday practice.
home.introText: Imagine Jeopardy, but with two teams of four. Anything and everything comes up!

quiz.question: This composer wrote the opera 'The Magic Flute' and Symphony No. 41, nicknamed the 'Jupiter.'
After surviving Captain Cook's voyage, Dartmouth's John Ledyard accidentally poisoned himself by this river where his lost grave is said to lie.
Because of local federalist tendencies, Dartmouth's cemetery does NOT contain the final resting place of a veteran from this early American conflict.
Dartmouth is home to the nation's oldest local fraternity, Kappa Pi Kappa, founded in what year?

The voice in Dartmouth's motto, "Vox Clamatis in Deserto", alludes to this biblical figure. Stained glass windows depict Eleazer Wheelock dressed as this figure in both Rollins chapel and a bathroom in Bartlett Hall.
A cannon from this conflict was found under the football bleachers in 2008 after a Dartmouth alumnus confessed a 1960's theft from a Vermont veterans' home.
This figure wrote in a letter to Dartmouth's then president: "I cannot but hope the institution will become still more flourishing and extensively useful."
The famous poet "Bobby" Frost was a member of which Dartmouth fraternity?
Dartmouth Hall has burned down this many times.
The Samson Occom and Eleazar Wheelock were two "Liberty" cargo ships in this American conflict.
75 disgruntled Dartmouth students completely demolished what campus icon in what year?
This element, atomic number 79, is represented by the symbol Au, derived from the Latin 'aurum.'
This author wrote 'Crime and Punishment' and 'The Brothers Karamazov,' two cornerstones of Russian literature.
The Battle of Hastings in 1066 resulted in the conquest of England by this duke from northern France.
This painter, known for 'The Persistence of Memory,' was a leading figure in the Surrealist movement.
In chemistry, this law states that the pressure of a gas is inversely proportional to its volume at constant temperature.
This Shakespeare play features the characters Rosencrantz and Guildenstern, childhood friends of the Danish prince.
The Pythagorean theorem applies to this specific type of triangle, relating the squares of its three sides.

current.name: William Parocai
current.title: President
current.bio: Hello, my name is Will ‘27 I am a Government and Earth Sciences Major and the President of Dartmouth Quiz Bowl. I am a generalist and my favourite topic is US/Canadian Geography. (DO NOT remove my British spelling convention)

current.name: Audrey Evans
current.title: Vice President
current.bio: Audrey is mostly known for powering music tossups and forgetting to send weekly emails.

current.name: Elliott Gomes
current.title: Secretary
current.bio: How many L's? How many T's? All I know is Gomes brings that party (because he has yet to send me a blurb).

current.name: Rajiv Pujara
current.title: Treasurer
current.bio: Rajiv is a '29 studying Economics, with Quiz Bowl specialties in Visual Fine Arts and Philosophy. He loves stopping by The Everyday Cafe before tournaments!

alumni.name: Jacob Markus '26
alumni.title: Ex-President (2024-26), "The Pentagon"
alumni.bio: Jake enjoys long walks, history tossups, and "antifloccinaucinihilipilification." Also Seattle, medtech, Chopin, golf, silly pictures with friends, and not knowing what birds are called. I wrote this website, which grants me a remarkable ability to break the 4th wall: hello reader, you should know life is good and so is Quizbowl.

alumni.name: Alexander Clarke '25
alumni.title: Founder, Ex-President (2021-25)
alumni.bio: Alexander Clarke '25 founded the Dartmouth Student Quizbowl Team in Fall 2021 and served as President for the next four years. Before he graduated, his interests included classical literature (though he still hasn't learned Latin or Ancient Greek), European history, and evolutionary biology. Though he now plays trivia as a graduate student at Yale, he is still a loyal child of the College on the Hill and is recognized as the Quizbowl Team's First Consul for Life.
